headlines.title: Rosneft Proposes Oil Firms to Refine 30% of Crude in Russia to Ease Fuel Crunch
headlines.summary: Russia should require its oil companies to refine at least 30% of their crude domestically to ease the fuel supply crunch, according to Igor Sechin, chief executive of Russia’s state-controlled firm Rosneft, which is the biggest oil producer in the country. headlines.title: Moscow Refinery May Stay Offline Until 2027
headlines.summary: The Moscow Oil Refinery is highly unlikely to resume production before 2027 after suffering extensive structural damage from multiple strikes by Ukraine’s long-range drones, dealing a further blow to a country grappling with severe fuel shortages, Reuters reports. headlines.title: U.S. Crude Inventories Post Another Major Draw
headlines.summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States decreased by 6.1 million barrels during the week ending June 19, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. headlines.title: UK Climate Panel Urges Faster Electrification to Lower Energy Bills

headlines.title: Euro Sinks To One-Year Low As Oil Price Drop Fuels ECB Rate Cut Bets
